title: alnus spachii
intro: Alnus spachii, also known as Spach's alder, is a species of flowering plant in the family Betulaceae. It is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to the mountainous regions of the Mediterranean. The bark and leaves of Alnus spachii have been used in traditional medicine for their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been found to have various health benefits, including reducing fever and soothing skin irritations. Alnus spachii can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of ailments, including respiratory issues and digestive problems.
